mnemonics: C-TIP for Computing Environments: Client-Server, Traditional, IoT, Pervasive/Cloud
keyPoints: A computing environment defines how and where computational tasks are performed and resources are managed.
Environments range from traditional (single machine) to distributed (client-server, cloud) and specialized (IoT, Real-Time).
The choice of environment dictates the operating system's design, focusing on aspects like resource management, security, and connectivity.
commonMistakes: Assuming a 'distributed system' is just a network of computers, rather than a system that appears as a single, coherent machine to the user.
Confusing 'Real-Time' systems (which have strict timing deadlines) with simply 'fast' systems.
Equating 'Cloud Computing' solely with data storage, overlooking its vast service models like IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS.
